title: Photos: Highlights from the St. Patrick’s Day Parade
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: The St. Patrick's Day Parade in South Boston started from Andrew Square on March 15, 2026.
Participants included the Fascinators of Tampa Bay with marchers like Kathy Luginbuel and Teresa Rogers.
Local Scott Poce added to the festivity by painting his eyebrows green for the parade event.

title: 2 men arrested after fatal overdose on Cape Cod prompts monthslong, multi-town investigation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: William Sarnie and Andre Silvia arrested for trafficking over 200 grams of cocaine on Cape Cod.
Investigation originated from a November 2025 fatal overdose linked to cocaine intoxication.
Sarnie held without bail; Silvia's arraignment pending; both face multiple drug-related charges.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Harry Styles hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Harry Styles hosted and performed on 'Saturday Night Live' on March 14, 2026, marking his second time in dual roles.
Ryan Gosling made a special cameo during Styles' musical guest performance introducing 'Dance No More' from Styles' new album.
'SNL's 15th episode of season 51 featured skits, Styles' monologue, and his musical performances including the weekend update segment.

title: Former Boston Public Schools teacher sentenced to 10 years in prison for child exploitation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Magee Gavin, 35, a former Boston teacher, was sentenced to 10 years in prison for child exploitation.
Gavin pleaded guilty to coercion, enticement of a minor, and child pornography charges, with five years supervised release.
Authorities identified Gavin's Discord account used to contact minors in the US, Canada, and the UK with explicit content.

title: 5 unsigned free agents that would be possible fits for Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Tyreek Hill is considered a high-risk, high-reward option for the Patriots if he recovers well from injury.
Marquise Brown, known for speed, could complement New England's receiver room if added alongside Romeo Doubs.
Linebacker Bobby Okereke could strengthen the Patriots' defense, having recorded 143 tackles and 6 passes defended in 2025.

title: Neemias Queta dominates in Celtics win over Wizards: 7 takeaways
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Neemias Queta led the Celtics with 24 points and 10 rebounds in their 111-100 victory over the Washington Wizards.
Jayson Tatum played 32 minutes, scoring 20 points, but struggled with his 3-point shooting, making only 2 of 9 attempts.
Luka Garza contributed 15 points in 15 minutes, shooting 83% from the field, during the Celtics' win against the Wizards.

title: Photos: Highlights from Dropkick Murphys’ St. Patrick’s Day kickoff at MGM Music Hall
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys kicked off their St. Patrick's Day weekend with a show on March 13 at MGM Music Hall.
The concert featured opening acts The Vigilantes, Haywire, and Showcase Showdown, each performing Friday night.
Dropkick Murphys lead singer Ken Casey engaged with the crowd, alongside band members playing iconic instruments.

title: ‘Feisty I think is the word’: The Boston vs. New York rivalry arrives in NWSL for Legacy FC’s debut
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC made their NWSL debut with a spirited 1-0 loss against Gotham FC at Gillette Stadium.
The match set an NWSL attendance record, drawing over 30,000 fans, and featured eight yellow cards.
Legacy FC's aggressive play resulted in a red card for Bianca St-Georges and challenges with possession.

title: Review and setlist: Dropkick Murphys fires up crowd, flames ICE at St. Paddy’s Day kickoff show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys kicked off St. Patrick's Day weekend with a concert at MGM Music Hall at Fenway on March 13, 2026.
The band played a mix of old and new songs, including tracks from their 1998 album and latest album 'For the People.'
The set included a political protest against ICE, with 'Citizen I.C.E.' featuring footage of ICE arrests displayed on stage.

title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, runs the Boston Marathon for Team Beth Israel to support Joslin Diabetes Center.
Bennett was diagnosed with Type 1 Diabetes at age 11 and credits Joslin for vital support.
He aims to inspire others with diabetes and run for a cure for millions affected by the disease.
